# Implementation: `web_search` Built-in Tool (Tavily)

**Spec:** `docs/plans/026_web_search_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
**Created:** 2026-03-05

---

## Phase 1: Tool Name Constant & Validation (no dependencies)

- [ ] Add `WebSearch = "web_search"` constant to the `const` block in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.1)*
- [ ] Add `WebSearch: true` entry to the map returned by `ValidNames()` in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.2)*
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: change expected count from 6 to 7 *(Req 5.1)*
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add `WebSearch` to the `expected` slice *(Req 5.2)*
- [ ] Update `TestConstants_Values` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add table entry `{"WebSearch", WebSearch, "web_search"}` *(Req 5.3)*
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/toolname/` package

---

## Phase 2: Web Search Tool — Types & Entry Function (depends on Phase 1)

- [ ] Create file `internal/tool/web_search.go` *(Req 2.1)*
- [ ] Define unexported request struct with JSON tags for: `query`, `max_results`, `search_depth`, `api_key` *(Req 3.6)*
- [ ] Define unexported response struct with `Results` field (slice of result structs) *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] Define unexported result struct with `Title`, `URL`, `Content` fields and JSON tags *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] Define package-level constants: `tavilyDefaultURL`, `tavilyMaxResults`, `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, `tavilyErrorBodyMax` *(Req 3.5, 3.6, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12)*
- [ ] Define unexported function `webSearchEntry() ToolEntry` that returns a `ToolEntry` with non-nil `Definition` and `Execute` *(Req 2.2)*
- [ ] `Definition` function returns tool with `Name: toolname.WebSearch` (not hardcoded) *(Req 2.3, 2.4)*
- [ ] `Definition` includes description explaining web search via Tavily returning titles, URLs, snippets *(Req 2.3)*
- [ ] `Definition` has one parameter: `query` (type `"string"`, required `true`, with description) *(Req 2.3, 2.5)*
- [ ] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 3: Web Search Tool — Executor Logic (depends on Phase 2)

- [ ] `Execute` function signature: `func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult` *(Req 3.1)*
- [ ] Extract `query` from `call.Arguments["query"]` *(Req 3.2)*
- [ ] Return error `ToolResult` if `query` is empty string: `"query is required"` *(Req 3.3)*
- [ ] Read API key via `os.Getenv("TAVILY_API_KEY")`; return error if empty: `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"` *(Req 3.4)*
- [ ] Read base URL via `os.Getenv("AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL")`; fall back to `tavilyDefaultURL` if empty *(Req 3.5)*
- [ ] Marshal request body using `json.Marshal` with the unexported request struct *(Req 3.6)*
- [ ] Create HTTP request with `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", baseURL+"/search", bytes.NewReader(body))` *(Req 3.7)*
- [ ] Set headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` *(Req 3.7)*
- [ ] Execute request with `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` *(Req 3.8)*
- [ ] Defer `resp.Body.Close()` immediately after successful `Do()` *(Req 3.9)*
- [ ] Read response body with `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, tavilyMaxResponseBytes+1))` *(Req 3.10)*
- [ ] If read bytes length > `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, return error: `"Tavily response too large to process"` *(Req 3.11)*
- [ ] If status code is non-2xx, return error: `fmt.Sprintf("Tavily API error (HTTP %d): %s", ...)` with body truncated to 500 bytes *(Req 3.12)*
- [ ] Unmarshal response body into response struct; return error with `"failed to parse Tavily response"` on failure *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] If results slice is empty/nil, return success with `"No results found."` *(Req 3.15)*
- [ ] Format results using `strings.Builder`: `Title: <title>\nURL: <url>\nSnippet: <content>` separated by `\n\n` *(Req 3.14)*
- [ ] Return success `ToolResult` with formatted content *(Req 3.16)*
- [ ] All error/success `ToolResult`s set `CallID: call.ID` *(Req 3.3–3.16)*
- [ ] Add deferred `toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.WebSearch, result, summary)` call *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Summary includes query truncated to 80 chars if longer *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Summary must NOT include API key *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 4: Registration (depends on Phase 3)

- [ ] Add `r.Register(toolname.WebSearch, webSearchEntry())` to `RegisterAll` in `internal/tool/registry.go` *(Req 4.1)*
- [ ] Verify: no other changes to `registry.go` *(Req 4.2)*

---

## Phase 5: Scaffold Update (no dependency on Phase 2–4)

- [ ] Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go` tools comment to: `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch, web_search` *(Req 6.1)*

---

## Phase 6: Tests — Toolname (depends on Phase 1)

Write tests RED first, then verify GREEN after Phase 1 code is in place.

- [ ] `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` — asserts count is 7
- [ ] `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` — includes `WebSearch`
- [ ] `TestConstants_Values` — includes `{"WebSearch", WebSearch, "web_search"}`
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/toolname/`

---

## Phase 7: Tests — Registry (depends on Phase 4)

- [ ] `TestRegisterAll_RegistersWebSearch` — `r.Has(toolname.WebSearch)` returns true *(Spec 7.4)*
- [ ] `TestRegisterAll_ResolvesWebSearch` — resolved tool has `Name == toolname.WebSearch` with `query` parameter *(Spec 7.4)*
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/tool/` registry tests

---

## Phase 8: Tests — Web Search Core (depends on Phase 3)

Write each test RED, then verify GREEN. Use `httptest.NewServer`, `t.Setenv`, real `Execute` calls.

### Input Validation
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_EmptyQuery` — empty Arguments map → `"query is required"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_MissingQueryArgument` — `{"query": ""}` → `"query is required"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_MissingAPIKey` — `TAVILY_API_KEY=""` → `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Success Path
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_Success` — 2 results, verify formatted output, `IsError: false`, `CallID` match *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_CallIDPassthrough` — `call.ID = "ws-unique-42"` → `ToolResult.CallID == "ws-unique-42"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Empty/Null Results
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_EmptyResults` — `{"results": []}` → `"No results found."`, `IsError: false` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_NullResults` — `{"results": null}` → `"No results found."`, `IsError: false` *(Spec 7.2)*

### HTTP Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_401` — status 401 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 401)"`, contains `"Invalid API key"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_429` — status 429 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 429)"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_500` — status 500 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 500)"`, contains `"internal error"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Parse Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_InvalidJSON` — `"not json"` with 200 → `"failed to parse Tavily response"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Network Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ContextCancellation` — handler blocks 10s, context timeout 50ms → `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ConnectionRefused` — closed port → `IsError: true`, non-empty content *(Spec 7.2)*

### Request Verification
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_BaseURLOverride` — verify server receives POST to `/search` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_RequestBody` — verify body contains `query`, `max_results: 10`, `search_depth: "basic"`, `api_key` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_AuthorizationHeader` — verify header `"Bearer test-key-123"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Verbose Logging
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_VerboseLog` — `Verbose: true`, buffer contains query, does NOT contain API key *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_VerboseLogQueryTruncation` — query >80 chars, logged summary is truncated *(Spec 7.2)*

### Safety Limits
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_LargeResponseTruncation` — body >102400 bytes → `"Tavily response too large to process"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ErrorBodyTruncation` — status 400, body >500 bytes → error body portion ≤500 bytes *(Spec 7.2)*

---

## Phase 9: Final Verification

- [ ] `make test` exits 0 — all existing tests pass unmodified (except toolname_test.go updates)
- [ ] `go build ./internal/tool/` succeeds
- [ ] `go build ./...` succeeds
- [ ] No new external dependencies in `go.mod`
- [ ] No changes to `cmd/run.go` or `internal/tool/tool.go`
- [ ] No changes to `internal/provider/` packages
- [ ] `call_agent` is NOT registered in the registry
- [ ] Scaffold comment includes `web_search`

---

## Constraints Checklist

- [ ] No new external dependencies (`go.mod` unchanged) *(Constraint 1)*
- [ ] No changes to `internal/provider/` *(Constraint 2)*
- [ ] Only modified files outside new ones: `registry.go` (1 line), `toolname.go`, `toolname_test.go`, `agent.go`, `registry_test.go` *(Constraint 3)*
- [ ] `call_agent` stays outside registry *(Constraint 4)*
- [ ] No path validation used *(Constraint 5)*
- [ ] `query` param is string from `map[string]string` *(Constraint 6)*
- [ ] `tavilyMaxResults = 10` is a constant *(Constraint 7)*
- [ ] `tavilyMaxResponseBytes = 102400` is a constant *(Constraint 8)*
- [ ] `tavilyErrorBodyMax = 500` is a constant *(Constraint 9)*
- [ ] API key via `os.Getenv`, not `config.ResolveAPIKey` *(Constraint 10)*
- [ ] No custom User-Agent *(Constraint 11)*
- [ ] No retry logic *(Constraint 12)*

# Implementation Instructions

1. Study the spec markdown file thoroughly
    - located in `./docs/plans/`
    - Use the spec file that starts with 026
    - If 026 is empty, blank, or does not exist; the newest file formatted as `xxx_topic_spec.md`
2. Study implementation plan markdown file thoroughly
    - located in `./docs/plans/`
    - Use the spec file that starts with 026
    - If 026 is empty, blank, or does not exist; the newest file formatted as `xxx_topic_implement.md`
3. Pick the highest leverage unchecked task
4. Complete the task
5. Verify the changes against `## Rules and Checks` listed below.
5. Update the implementation plan to check off the task you just completed
6. After completeing a single phase, run the `auto-review` skill.
7, After the review changes are finished, run `git diff --shortstat` to check the total line changes.
    - If the changes are greater than 500 stop implementing and let the human know.
    - If the changes are less than 500 continue to the next phase of the implementation plan
8. Repeat steps 3-7 until all phases are complete.

## Rules

- Use agents for exploring the codebase
    - Each agent must only have one explicit task.
    - Each agent must only return the information that fulfills that task.
    - Spin off multiple agents in parallel for tasks that do not depend on another task.
- Use red/greed TDD
- If there are any SQL/ORM changes, please compare them against working queries in the codebase for syntax correctness.
- Verify that the unit tests for functions with updates have a test case to that will verify the change.
- Verify that all new code has an unbiased unit test.
- Verify that no import paths were changed for files in the `/lambdas` directory.
    - If tests require different import resolution than production, fix the test infrastructure (conftest.py, pytest.ini) without modifying the Lambda source imports.
- If there was an attempt at fixing a pattern, you must make a comprehensive search proving ALL instances are addressed.

<content>1: # Specification: `web_search` Built-in Tool (Tavily)
2: 
3: **Status:** Draft
4: **Version:** 1.0
5: **Created:** 2026-03-05
6: **GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
7: **Scope:** Web search tool using Tavily Search API with JSON request/response, API key from environment, and formatted text output
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## 1. Purpose
12: 
13: Implement the `web_search` tool — a built-in tool registered in the `Registry` that performs web searches via the Tavily Search API and returns results as formatted text (titles, URLs, snippets). This tool is needed for feature parity when axe replaces the custom container worker in Agent Engine.
14: 
15: This tool follows the same pattern as the M3-M8 tools (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`):
16: 
17: - **`RegisterAll`** — extended with one additional `r.Register(...)` call
18: - **`toolname.WebSearch`** — new constant declared in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`
19: - **`ExecContext`** — reused for `Stderr` and `Verbose` (verbose logging). `Workdir` is not used by this tool.
20: 
21: This tool does NOT use `validatePath` or `isWithinDir`. It operates on the Tavily API, not file paths. There is no filesystem interaction.
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ## 2. Design Decisions
26: 
27: The following decisions were made during planning and are binding for implementation:
28: 
29: 1. **Tavily Search API.** The tool sends a POST request to the Tavily Search API endpoint (`/search`). No other Tavily endpoints (extract, map, crawl) are used.
30: 
31: 2. **Single parameter: `query`.** The tool has one required parameter named `query` of type `string`. There are no optional parameters. The `query` string is passed directly to the Tavily API's `query` field.
32: 
33: 3. **API key from environment variable.** The Tavily API key is read from the `TAVILY_API_KEY` environment variable via `os.Getenv("TAVILY_API_KEY")` at execution time. If the variable is empty or unset, the tool returns an error. The API key is NOT resolved through `config.ResolveAPIKey` — Tavily is not an LLM provider; it is a tool dependency.
34: 
35: 4. **Bearer token authentication.** The API key is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_key>`, per Tavily's API documentation. It is also included in the JSON request body as the `api_key` field for backward compatibility with older Tavily API versions.
36: 
37: 5. **Base URL with environment override.** The default base URL is `https://api.tavily.com`. This can be overridden via the `AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL` environment variable (useful for testing or self-hosted instances). The URL check uses `os.Getenv("AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL")`.
38: 
39: 6. **10 results maximum.** The request includes `"max_results": 10` in the JSON body. This is a package-level constant, not configurable by the agent or user.
40: 
41: 7. **Basic search depth.** The request uses `"search_depth": "basic"`. Advanced search is not offered.
42: 
43: 8. **No images or videos.** The request sets `"include_images": false` and `"include_images": false`. Only text results are returned.
44: 
45: 9. **Formatted text output.** Each result is formatted as:
46:    ```
47:    Title: <title>
48:    URL: <url>
49:    Snippet: <content>
50:    ```
51:    Results are separated by a blank line (`\n\n`). If there are zero results, the output is the string `"No results found."`.
52: 
53: 10. **Response body read limit.** The response body from Tavily is read with a limit of 102400 bytes (100KB) via `io.LimitReader`. This prevents excessive memory usage from unexpectedly large API responses. If the response exceeds this limit, the raw body is truncated and the tool returns an error indicating the response was too large to parse.
54: 
55: 11. **HTTP error handling.** Non-2xx status codes from the Tavily API return `ToolResult{IsError: true}` with content in the format `"Tavily API error (HTTP %d): %s"` where the first value is the status code and the second is the response body (truncated to the first 500 bytes for readability).
56: 
57: 12. **JSON parse errors.** If the Tavily API returns a 2xx response that cannot be parsed as JSON, the tool returns `ToolResult{IsError: true}` with a message containing `"failed to parse Tavily response"` and the underlying error.
58: 
59: 13. **Context-based timeout.** The tool creates an `http.Request` using `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", url, body)`. The `ctx` parameter from the executor signature is passed directly. The tool inherits whatever timeout the caller provides.
60: 
61: 14. **`http.DefaultClient` for execution.** The tool uses `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` to execute the request. No custom transport.
62: 
63: 15. **No retry logic.** A single request is made. If it fails, the error is returned.
64: 
65: 16. **`call_agent` remains outside the registry.** Same as all prior tool milestones.
66: 
67: 17. **No new external dependencies.** Uses only Go stdlib (`net/http`, `encoding/json`, `io`, `fmt`, `context`, `os`, `bytes`, `strings`).

98: ### 3.3 `web_search` Tool Executor
99: 
100: **Requirement 3.1:** The `Execute` function must have the signature: `func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult`.
101: 
102: **Requirement 3.2:** Extract the `query` argument from `call.Arguments["query"]`.
103: 
104: **Requirement 3.3 (Empty Query Check):** If `query` is an empty string (including when the key is absent from the map), return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "query is required"`, `IsError: true`.
105: 
106: **Requirement 3.4 (API Key Check):** Read the API key from `os.Getenv("TAVILY_API_KEY")`. If the value is an empty string, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"`, `IsError: true`.
107: 
108: **Requirement 3.5 (Base URL Resolution):** Read the base URL from `os.Getenv("AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL")`. If the value is an empty string, use the default `"https://api.tavily.com"`. The default URL must be a package-level constant named `tavilyDefaultURL`.
109: 
110: **Requirement 3.6 (Build Request Body):** Construct a JSON request body with the following fields:
111: - `"query"`: the query string from the tool call argument
112: - `"max_results"`: the integer 10 (package-level constant `tavilyMaxResults`)
113: - `"search_depth"`: the string `"basic"`
114: - `"api_key"`: the API key string
115: 
116: The request body must be serialized using `encoding/json.Marshal()`. The struct used for marshaling must be an unexported type defined in the same file. If marshaling fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
117: 
118: **Requirement 3.7 (Create HTTP Request):** Create an HTTP request using `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", baseURL+"/search", bytes.NewReader(body))`. Set the following headers:
119: - `Content-Type`: `"application/json"`
120: - `Authorization`: `"Bearer " + apiKey`
121: 
122: If request creation fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
123: 
124: **Requirement 3.8 (Execute Request):** Execute the request using `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`. If execution fails (network error, DNS failure, TLS error, context cancellation), return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
125: 
126: **Requirement 3.9 (Close Response Body):** Immediately after a successful `Do()` call, defer `resp.Body.Close()`.
127: 
128: **Requirement 3.10 (Read Response Body):** Read the response body using `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, tavilyMaxResponseBytes+1))` where `tavilyMaxResponseBytes` is 102400 (100KB, a package-level constant). If reading fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
129: 
130: **Requirement 3.11 (Response Too Large):** If the length of the read byte slice exceeds `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "Tavily response too large to process"`, `IsError: true`.
131: 
132: **Requirement 3.12 (Non-2xx Status):** If `resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300`, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content` in the format `"Tavily API error (HTTP %d): %s"` where the first value is `resp.StatusCode` and the second value is the response body truncated to the first 500 bytes. `IsError: true`.
133: 
134: **Requirement 3.13 (Parse JSON Response):** Unmarshal the response body into a struct containing a `Results` field (slice of result structs). Each result struct must have `Title string`, `URL string`, and `Content string` fields with appropriate `json` tags. The struct types must be unexported. If unmarshaling fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content` containing `"failed to parse Tavily response"` and the error message, `IsError: true`.
135: 
136: **Requirement 3.14 (Format Results):** Iterate over the results slice. For each result, format as:
137: ```
138: Title: <title>
139: URL: <url>
140: Snippet: <content>
141: ```
142: Separate results with `"\n\n"`. Use `strings.Builder` for concatenation. Return the final string trimmed of trailing whitespace.
143: 
144: **Requirement 3.15 (Empty Results):** If the results slice is empty (length 0) or nil, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "No results found."`, `IsError: false`.
145: 
146: **Requirement 3.16 (Success Case):** Return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content` containing the formatted results string, `IsError: false`.

148: **Requirement 3.17 (Verbose Logging):** Use `toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.WebSearch, result, summary)` via a deferred function, consistent with all other tools. The summary must include the query (truncated to 80 characters if longer). The summary must NOT include the API key.

206: ## 5. Edge Cases
207: 
208: ### 5.1 Query Argument
209: 
210: | Scenario | Input `query` | Behavior |
211: |----------|--------------|----------|
212: | Empty query | `""` | Error: `"query is required"` |
213: | Missing `query` key in Arguments map | Key absent | `call.Arguments["query"]` returns `""` -> Error: `"query is required"` |
214: | Valid query | `"golang concurrency patterns"` | Normal API call. Returns formatted results. |
215: | Very long query | 10,000+ character string | Passed to Tavily API. Tavily may reject it; tool reports the server's response. |
216: | Query with special characters | `"what is 2+2?"` | Passed as-is in JSON body. JSON marshaling handles escaping. |
217: | Whitespace-only query | `"   "` | Passed to Tavily API. Not special-cased — the tool does not trim whitespace. Tavily's handling determines the outcome. |
218: 
219: ### 5.2 API Key
220: 
221: | Scenario | `TAVILY_API_KEY` env var | Behavior |
222: |----------|------------------------|----------|
223: | Not set | Absent | Error: `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"` |
224: | Empty string | `""` | Error: `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"` |
225: | Valid key | `"tvly-abc123..."` | Normal API call. Key sent in Authorization header and request body. |
226: | Invalid key | `"invalid-key"` | Tavily returns HTTP 401. Tool reports: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 401): ..."`. |
227: | Key with whitespace | `" tvly-abc123 "` | Passed as-is. Leading/trailing whitespace is not trimmed. Tavily may reject. |
228: 
229: ### 5.3 Base URL
230: 
231: | Scenario | `AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL` env var | Behavior |
232: |----------|------------------------------|----------|
233: | Not set | Absent | Uses default `"https://api.tavily.com"` |
234: | Empty string | `""` | Uses default `"https://api.tavily.com"` |
235: | Custom URL | `"http://localhost:8080"` | POST to `http://localhost:8080/search` |
236: | URL with trailing slash | `"https://api.tavily.com/"` | POST to `"https://api.tavily.com//search"` (double slash). Not corrected — the user must provide a clean URL. |
237: 
238: ### 5.4 Tavily API HTTP Status Codes
239: 
240: | Scenario | Status Code | Behavior |
241: |----------|------------|----------|
242: | 200 OK | 200 | Parse JSON, format results, `IsError: false`. |
243: | 400 Bad Request | 400 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 400): <body>"`. |
244: | 401 Unauthorized | 401 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 401): <body>"`. Invalid or missing API key. |
245: | 429 Too Many Requests | 429 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 429): <body>"`. Rate limit exceeded. |
246: | 432 Key/Plan Limit Exceeded | 432 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 432): <body>"`. |
247: | 433 PayGo Limit Exceeded | 433 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 433): <body>"`. |
248: | 500 Internal Server Error | 500 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"Tavily API error (HTTP 500): <body>"`. |
249: 
250: ### 5.5 Tavily API Response Parsing
251: 
252: | Scenario | Response body | Behavior |
253: |----------|--------------|----------|
254: | Normal results | `{"results": [{"title": "T", "url": "U", "content": "C"}]}` | Formatted output with title, URL, snippet. |
255: | Empty results array | `{"results": []}` | `"No results found."` with `IsError: false`. |
256: | Null results field | `{"results": null}` | `"No results found."` with `IsError: false`. |
257: | Missing results field | `{}` | `"No results found."` with `IsError: false` (JSON unmarshal leaves slice as nil). |
258: | Invalid JSON | `not json` | Error: `"failed to parse Tavily response: ..."`. |
259: | Extra fields in response | `{"results": [...], "usage": {...}, "request_id": "..."}` | Extra fields are ignored. Only `results` is extracted. |
260: | Extra fields in result objects | `{"results": [{"title": "T", "url": "U", "content": "C", "score": 0.95}]}` | Extra fields (`score`, `images`, `videos`) are ignored. Only `title`, `url`, `content` are used. |
261: | Result with empty title | `{"results": [{"title": "", "url": "U", "content": "C"}]}` | Formatted as `Title: \nURL: U\nSnippet: C`. Empty fields are not special-cased. |
262: | Response body > 100KB | Very large JSON | Error: `"Tavily response too large to process"`. |
263: 
264: ### 5.6 Network Errors
265: 
266: | Scenario | Behavior |
267: |----------|----------|
268: | DNS resolution failure | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
269: | Connection refused | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
270: | Connection timeout | `Do()` returns an error (via context deadline). `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
271: | TLS certificate error | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
272: | Context cancelled before request | `Do()` returns context error immediately. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
273: | Context cancelled during response read | `io.ReadAll` returns a context error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |

309: Tests must follow the patterns established in the existing tool tests:

311: - **Package-level tests:** Tests live in the same package (`package tool`)

316: - **Test real code, not mocks.** Tests must call the actual `webSearchExecute` function through the `ToolEntry.Execute` function pointer. Each test must fail if the code under test is deleted.
317: - **Red/green TDD:** Write failing tests first, then implement code to make them pass.
318: - **Run tests with:** `make test`

444: ## 9. Out of Scope
445: 
446: The following items are explicitly **not** included in this milestone:
447: 
448: 1. Tavily Extract endpoint (`/extract`)
449: 2. Tavily Map endpoint (`/map`)

452: 5. Configurable `max_results` (always 10)
453: 6. Image or video results (`include_images`, `include_videos` always false)
454: 7. Domain filtering (`include_domains`, `exclude_domains`)
455: 8. Pagination / `next_page` handling
456: 9. Response caching
457: 10. Retry logic or exponential backoff
458: 11. API key resolution through `config.toml` or `config.ResolveAPIKey`
459: 12. Storing Tavily config in `config.toml` provider section

461: 14. Custom User-Agent header
462: 15. Rate limiting or request throttling
463: 16. Streaming response output
464: 17. Result scoring or filtering by relevance score
465: 18. HTML content extraction or conversion
466: 19. Changes to `--dry-run`, `--json`, or `--verbose` output formatting (existing patterns already handle new tools)
467: 20. Changes to `cmd/run.go` or `internal/tool/tool.go`
468: 
469: ---
470: 
471: ## 10. References
472: 
473: - GitHub Issue: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
474: - Tavily Search API: `POST https://api.tavily.com/search`
475: - Tavily API Authentication: Bearer token via `Authorization` header
476: - url_fetch Tool Spec (pattern reference): `docs/plans/025_url_fetch_spec.md`
477: - `RegisterAll` function: `internal/tool/registry.go:92`
478: - `ValidNames` function: `internal/toolname/toolname.go:19`
479: - `provider.Tool` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:34`
480: - `provider.ToolCall` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:41`
481: - `provider.ToolResult` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:48`
482: - `ToolEntry` type: `internal/tool/registry.go:20`
483: - `ExecContext` type: `internal/tool/registry.go:13`
484: - `toolVerboseLog` helper: `internal/tool/verbose.go:11`
485: - `Scaffold` function: `internal/agent/agent.go:157`
486: - Agent config `tools` field: `internal/agent/agent.go:45`
487: - Tool call milestones: `docs/plans/000_tool_call_milestones.md`
488: 
489: ---
490: 
491: ## 11. Notes
492: 
493: - **No `config.ResolveAPIKey` usage.** Tavily is not an LLM provider — it's a tool dependency. LLM providers (anthropic, openai, ollama) use the `config.ResolveAPIKey` chain (env var > config.toml). Tavily's key is read directly from `TAVILY_API_KEY` because there is no reason to store it in `config.toml` alongside LLM provider keys. If future tools also need API keys, this pattern can be revisited.
494: - **`api_key` in both header and body.** The Tavily API documentation shows two authentication methods: Bearer token in the `Authorization` header (current docs) and `api_key` field in the JSON body (older docs/quickstart). Including both ensures compatibility across Tavily API versions.
495: - **100KB response limit.** The Tavily API typically returns small JSON payloads (a few KB for 10 results). The 100KB limit is a safety rail, not a practical constraint. It prevents memory issues if the API behavior changes or returns unexpected data.
496: - **500-byte error body truncation.** Tavily error responses are typically small JSON objects (`{"detail": {"error": "..."}}`). The 500-byte limit keeps error messages readable while preventing large error pages from flooding the tool result.
497: - **`io.LimitReader` for memory safety.** Without a read limit, a misbehaving or compromised API could send a multi-gigabyte response, causing OOM. `io.LimitReader` caps memory usage at `tavilyMaxResponseBytes+1` bytes.
498: - **No `Workdir` usage.** Unlike file tools, `web_search` does not use `ExecContext.Workdir`. The tool makes network API calls, not filesystem operations.
499: - **The `RegisterAll` pattern continues.** This adds the seventh tool: `list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`, `web_search`.
500: - **Security model.** The `web_search` tool allows the LLM to perform arbitrary web searches. This is by design — if an agent has `tools = ["web_search"]` in its TOML config, the user has explicitly opted in. The API key acts as the access control — no key means no searches. The tool never exposes the API key to the LLM (it's in the HTTP header/body, not in the tool result).

(End of file - total 500 lines)
</content>

# Specification: `web_search` Built-in Tool (Tavily)

**Status:** Draft
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-03-05
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
**Scope:** Web search tool using Tavily Search API with JSON request/response, API key from environment, and formatted text output

---

## 1. Purpose

Implement the `web_search` tool — a built-in tool registered in the `Registry` that performs web searches via the Tavily Search API and returns results as formatted text (titles, URLs, snippets). This tool is needed for feature parity when axe replaces the custom container worker in Agent Engine.

This tool follows the same pattern as the M3-M8 tools (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`):

- **`RegisterAll`** — extended with one additional `r.Register(...)` call
- **`toolname.WebSearch`** — new constant declared in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`
- **`ExecContext`** — reused for `Stderr` and `Verbose` (verbose logging). `Workdir` is not used by this tool.

# Implementation: `web_search` Built-in Tool (Tavily)

**Spec:** `docs/plans/026_web_search_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
**Created:** 2026-03-05

---

## Phase 1: Tool Name Constant & Validation (no dependencies)

- [ ] Add `WebSearch = "web_search"` constant to the `const` block in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.1)*
- [ ] Add `WebSearch: true` entry to the map returned by `ValidNames()` in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.2)*
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: change expected count from 6 to 7 *(Req 5.1)*
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add `WebSearch` to the `expected` slice *(Req 5.2)*
- [ ] Update `TestConstants_Values` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add table entry `{"WebSearch", WebSearch, "web_search"}` *(Req 5.3)*
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/toolname/` package

---

## Phase 2: Web Search Tool — Types & Entry Function (depends on Phase 1)

- [ ] Create file `internal/tool/web_search.go` *(Req 2.1)*
- [ ] Define unexported request struct with JSON tags for: `query`, `max_results`, `search_depth`, `api_key` *(Req 3.6)*
- [ ] Define unexported response struct with `Results` field (slice of result structs) *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] Define unexported result struct with `Title`, `URL`, `Content` fields and JSON tags *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] Define package-level constants: `tavilyDefaultURL`, `tavilyMaxResults`, `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, `tavilyErrorBodyMax` *(Req 3.5, 3.6, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12)*
- [ ] Define unexported function `webSearchEntry() ToolEntry` that returns a `ToolEntry` with non-nil `Definition` and `Execute` *(Req 2.2)*
- [ ] `Definition` function returns tool with `Name: toolname.WebSearch` (not hardcoded) *(Req 2.3, 2.4)*
- [ ] `Definition` includes description explaining web search via Tavily returning titles, URLs, snippets *(Req 2.3)*
- [ ] `Definition` has one parameter: `query` (type `"string"`, required `true`, with description) *(Req 2.3, 2.5)*
- [ ] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 3: Web Search Tool — Executor Logic (depends on Phase 2)

- [ ] `Execute` function signature: `func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult` *(Req 3.1)*
- [ ] Extract `query` from `call.Arguments["query"]` *(Req 3.2)*
- [ ] Return error `ToolResult` if `query` is empty string: `"query is required"` *(Req 3.3)*
- [ ] Read API key via `os.Getenv("TAVILY_API_KEY")`; return error if empty: `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"` *(Req 3.4)*
- [ ] Read base URL via `os.Getenv("AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL")`; fall back to `tavilyDefaultURL` if empty *(Req 3.5)*
- [ ] Marshal request body using `json.Marshal` with the unexported request struct *(Req 3.6)*
- [ ] Create HTTP request with `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", baseURL+"/search", bytes.NewReader(body))` *(Req 3.7)*
- [ ] Set headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` *(Req 3.7)*
- [ ] Execute request with `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` *(Req 3.8)*
- [ ] Defer `resp.Body.Close()` immediately after successful `Do()` *(Req 3.9)*
- [ ] Read response body with `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, tavilyMaxResponseBytes+1))` *(Req 3.10)*
- [ ] If read bytes length > `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, return error: `"Tavily response too large to process"` *(Req 3.11)*
- [ ] If status code is non-2xx, return error: `fmt.Sprintf("Tavily API error (HTTP %d): %s", ...)` with body truncated to 500 bytes *(Req 3.12)*
- [ ] Unmarshal response body into response struct; return error with `"failed to parse Tavily response"` on failure *(Req 3.13)*
- [ ] If results slice is empty/nil, return success with `"No results found."` *(Req 3.15)*
- [ ] Format results using `strings.Builder`: `Title: <title>\nURL: <url>\nSnippet: <content>` separated by `\n\n` *(Req 3.14)*
- [ ] Return success `ToolResult` with formatted content *(Req 3.16)*
- [ ] All error/success `ToolResult`s set `CallID: call.ID` *(Req 3.3–3.16)*
- [ ] Add deferred `toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.WebSearch, result, summary)` call *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Summary includes query truncated to 80 chars if longer *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Summary must NOT include API key *(Req 3.17)*
- [ ] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 4: Registration (depends on Phase 3)

- [ ] Add `r.Register(toolname.WebSearch, webSearchEntry())` to `RegisterAll` in `internal/tool/registry.go` *(Req 4.1)*
- [ ] Verify: no other changes to `registry.go` *(Req 4.2)*

---

## Phase 5: Scaffold Update (no dependency on Phase 2–4)

- [ ] Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go` tools comment to: `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch, web_search` *(Req 6.1)*

---

## Phase 6: Tests — Toolname (depends on Phase 1)

Write tests RED first, then verify GREEN after Phase 1 code is in place.

- [ ] `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` — asserts count is 7
- [ ] `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` — includes `WebSearch`
- [ ] `TestConstants_Values` — includes `{"WebSearch", WebSearch, "web_search"}`
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/toolname/`

---

## Phase 7: Tests — Registry (depends on Phase 4)

- [ ] `TestRegisterAll_RegistersWebSearch` — `r.Has(toolname.WebSearch)` returns true *(Spec 7.4)*
- [ ] `TestRegisterAll_ResolvesWebSearch` — resolved tool has `Name == toolname.WebSearch` with `query` parameter *(Spec 7.4)*
- [ ] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/tool/` registry tests

---

## Phase 8: Tests — Web Search Core (depends on Phase 3)

Write each test RED, then verify GREEN. Use `httptest.NewServer`, `t.Setenv`, real `Execute` calls.

### Input Validation
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_EmptyQuery` — empty Arguments map → `"query is required"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_MissingQueryArgument` — `{"query": ""}` → `"query is required"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_MissingAPIKey` — `TAVILY_API_KEY=""` → `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"`, `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Success Path
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_Success` — 2 results, verify formatted output, `IsError: false`, `CallID` match *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_CallIDPassthrough` — `call.ID = "ws-unique-42"` → `ToolResult.CallID == "ws-unique-42"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Empty/Null Results
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_EmptyResults` — `{"results": []}` → `"No results found."`, `IsError: false` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_NullResults` — `{"results": null}` → `"No results found."`, `IsError: false` *(Spec 7.2)*

### HTTP Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_401` — status 401 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 401)"`, contains `"Invalid API key"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_429` — status 429 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 429)"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_HTTPError_500` — status 500 → `"Tavily API error (HTTP 500)"`, contains `"internal error"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Parse Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_InvalidJSON` — `"not json"` with 200 → `"failed to parse Tavily response"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Network Errors
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ContextCancellation` — handler blocks 10s, context timeout 50ms → `IsError: true` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ConnectionRefused` — closed port → `IsError: true`, non-empty content *(Spec 7.2)*

### Request Verification
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_BaseURLOverride` — verify server receives POST to `/search` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_RequestBody` — verify body contains `query`, `max_results: 10`, `search_depth: "basic"`, `api_key` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_AuthorizationHeader` — verify header `"Bearer test-key-123"` *(Spec 7.2)*

### Verbose Logging
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_VerboseLog` — `Verbose: true`, buffer contains query, does NOT contain API key *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_VerboseLogQueryTruncation` — query >80 chars, logged summary is truncated *(Spec 7.2)*

### Safety Limits
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_LargeResponseTruncation` — body >102400 bytes → `"Tavily response too large to process"` *(Spec 7.2)*
- [ ] `TestWebSearch_ErrorBodyTruncation` — status 400, body >500 bytes → error body portion ≤500 bytes *(Spec 7.2)*

## Phase 9: Final Verification

- [ ] `make test` exits 0 — all existing tests pass unmodified (except toolname_test.go updates)
- [ ] `go build ./internal/tool/` succeeds
- [ ] `go build ./...` succeeds
- [ ] No new external dependencies in `go.mod`

- [ ] No changes to `internal/provider/` packages

- [ ] No new external dependencies (`go.mod` unchanged) *(Constraint 1)*
- [ ] No changes to `internal/provider/` *(Constraint 2)*
- [ ] Only modified files outside new ones: `registry.go` (1 line), `toolname.go`, `toolname_test.go`, `agent.go`, `registry_test.go` *(Constraint 3)*

# Implementation: `web_search` Built-in Tool (Tavily)

**Spec:** `docs/plans/026_web_search_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/6
**Created:** 2026-03-05

---

## Phase 1: Tool Name Constant & Validation (no dependencies)

- [x] Add `WebSearch = "web_search"` constant to the `const` block in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.1)*
- [x] Add `WebSearch: true` entry to the map returned by `ValidNames()` in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` *(Req 1.2)*
- [x] Update `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: change expected count from 6 to 7 *(Req 5.1)*
- [x] Update `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add `WebSearch` to the `expected` slice *(Req 5.2)*
- [x] Update `TestConstants_Values` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add table entry `{"WebSearch", WebSearch, "web_search"}` *(Req 5.3)*
- [x] Verify: `make test` passes for `internal/toolname/` package

---

## Phase 2: Web Search Tool — Types & Entry Function (depends on Phase 1)

- [x] Create file `internal/tool/web_search.go` *(Req 2.1)*
- [x] Define unexported request struct with JSON tags for: `query`, `max_results`, `search_depth`, `api_key` *(Req 3.6)*
- [x] Define unexported response struct with `Results` field (slice of result structs) *(Req 3.13)*
- [x] Define unexported result struct with `Title`, `URL`, `Content` fields and JSON tags *(Req 3.13)*
- [x] Define package-level constants: `tavilyDefaultURL`, `tavilyMaxResults`, `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, `tavilyErrorBodyMax` *(Req 3.5, 3.6, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12)*
- [x] Define unexported function `webSearchEntry() ToolEntry` that returns a `ToolEntry` with non-nil `Definition` and `Execute` *(Req 2.2)*
- [x] `Definition` function returns tool with `Name: toolname.WebSearch` (not hardcoded) *(Req 2.3, 2.4)*
- [x] `Definition` includes description explaining web search via Tavily returning titles, URLs, snippets *(Req 2.3)*
- [x] `Definition` has one parameter: `query` (type `"string"`, required `true`, with description) *(Req 2.3, 2.5)*
- [x] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 3: Web Search Tool — Executor Logic (depends on Phase 2)

- [x] `Execute` function signature: `func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult` *(Req 3.1)*
- [x] Extract `query` from `call.Arguments["query"]` *(Req 3.2)*
- [x] Return error `ToolResult` if `query` is empty string: `"query is required"` *(Req 3.3)*
- [x] Read API key via `os.Getenv("TAVILY_API_KEY")`; return error if empty: `"TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is not set"` *(Req 3.4)*
- [x] Read base URL via `os.Getenv("AXE_TAVILY_BASE_URL")`; fall back to `tavilyDefaultURL` if empty *(Req 3.5)*
- [x] Marshal request body using `json.Marshal` with the unexported request struct *(Req 3.6)*
- [x] Create HTTP request with `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", baseURL+"/search", bytes.NewReader(body))` *(Req 3.7)*
- [x] Set headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` *(Req 3.7)*
- [x] Execute request with `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` *(Req 3.8)*
- [x] Defer `resp.Body.Close()` immediately after successful `Do()` *(Req 3.9)*
- [x] Read response body with `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, tavilyMaxResponseBytes+1))` *(Req 3.10)*
- [x] If read bytes length > `tavilyMaxResponseBytes`, return error: `"Tavily response too large to process"` *(Req 3.11)*
- [x] If status code is non-2xx, return error: `fmt.Sprintf("Tavily API error (HTTP %d): %s", ...)` with body truncated to 500 bytes *(Req 3.12)*
- [x] Unmarshal response body into response struct; return error with `"failed to parse Tavily response"` on failure *(Req 3.13)*
- [x] If results slice is empty/nil, return success with `"No results found."` *(Req 3.15)*
- [x] Format results using `strings.Builder`: `Title: <title>\nURL: <url>\nSnippet: <content>` separated by `\n\n` *(Req 3.14)*
- [x] Return success `ToolResult` with formatted content *(Req 3.16)*
- [x] All error/success `ToolResult`s set `CallID: call.ID` *(Req 3.3–3.16)*
- [x] Add deferred `toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.WebSearch, result, summary)` call *(Req 3.17)*
- [x] Summary includes query truncated to 80 chars if longer *(Req 3.17)*
- [x] Summary must NOT include API key *(Req 3.17)*
- [x] Verify: `go build ./internal/tool/` compiles

---

## Phase 4: Registration (depends on Phase 3)

- [x] Add `r.Register(toolname.WebSearch, webSearchEntry())` to `RegisterAll` in `internal/tool/registry.go` *(Req 4.1)*
- [x] Verify: no other changes to `registry.go` *(Req 4.2)*
